supplemental_constraints.finding: Donor 1 showed net glucose production at 6 h in every tested condition except HI+HG; donor 2 showed no net glucose production at any studied time point.
supplemental_constraints.model_consequence: Model output must remain signed; a non-negative consumption clamp is prohibited, but no donor-specific numeric trajectory may be reconstructed from the figure caption.

supplemental_constraints.finding: ATP-assay viability before and after the 72 h glucose and hormone challenge was reported as not significantly different.
supplemental_constraints.model_consequence: The qualitative viability result cannot replace viable-cell counts at 0, 6, 24 and 72 h and cannot change the seeded-cell denominator.

quantity_audit.reason: The assay directly constrains signed net glucose amount lost from the medium per seeded cell over each reported time window.
Net medium disappearance does not separate inward from outward glucose transport.
Donor-level net production proves that outward glucose flux can contribute, but the pooled medium endpoint does not quantify it separately.
Extracellular net glucose change does not measure intracellular glucose phosphorylation.
Medium glucose alone cannot resolve glucose-6-phosphate dephosphorylation from transport or other glucose-producing routes.
Net glucose disappearance does not report what fraction enters glycogen.
Net production can arise from glycogenolysis or gluconeogenesis and the current endpoint does not distinguish them.
Medium disappearance does not distinguish glycolysis from glycogen synthesis, pentose-phosphate flux or intracellular accumulation.
A signed net endpoint cannot allocate glucose production between gluconeogenesis and glycogenolysis.
No isotopologue or intracellular product measurement separates pentose-phosphate use from other glucose fates.
The supplement establishes a donor-dependent sign difference qualitatively, while Table 1 pools the healthy insulin-sensitive records and does not expose a numeric donor trajectory.
Low-insulin media also contain 100 nM glucagon, whereas glucagon in high-insulin media is not reported as a measured zero.
The reported denominator is seeded cells; window-specific viable-cell counts are unavailable.

required_measurements.label: Signed donor-resolved medium mass balance
requirements: Donor identity and biological replicate identity
Glucose concentration at every challenge time point
Initial and remaining medium volume after every sample
Numeric volumetric factor used by the assay
Seeded and viable cell count at every window
required_measurements.purpose: Resolve donor trajectories and an exact amount balance without inventing a volume or cell-count correction.

required_measurements.label: Isotope-resolved fluxomics
requirements: A declared atom-mapped metabolic network and flux-identifiability analysis
required_measurements.purpose: Separate glucose uptake, production, recycling and pathway allocation.

required_measurements.label: Intracellular metabolite time course
required_measurements.purpose: Constrain intracellular storage and competing glucose fates.

required_measurements.label: Transporter and enzyme abundance
requirements: GCK and G6PC abundance or activity
required_measurements.purpose: Convert flux observations into identifiable capacity parameters instead of assumed enzyme concentrations.

required_measurements.label: Orthogonal hormone perturbation time course
requirements: Insulin varied while glucagon and glucose are held fixed
Glucagon varied while insulin and glucose are held fixed
INSR-AKT and cAMP-PKA response readouts with glucose flux
required_measurements.purpose: Estimate insulin- and glucagon-specific causal responses without exposure-bundle confounding.

limitations: The measurement operator transforms a model output into the published observation space; it is not a biological rate law and supplies no parameter values.
The Kemas aggregate endpoint is a signed net medium balance and cannot identify bidirectional transport or intracellular pathway fluxes by itself.
The Grankvist isotope-tracing study demonstrates a measurement strategy in intact human liver tissue, not a transferable numeric calibration for PHH spheroids.
No synthetic trajectory, fitted parameter, acceptance threshold or model-state coupling is exported.
